title: Implicit observed-only matvec must be derived and proved (avoid O(M) KWZ^T)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Gap G1 (central, Lemma L2).** The PCG matrix-vector product $u\mapsto\mathcal A u$, $u=\operatorname{vec}(W)$, must be implemented using ONLY the $q$ observed entries and the small factors $\tilde K\in\mathbb R^{q\times n}$, $\tilde Z\in\mathbb R^{q\times r}$, $K\in\mathbb R^{n\times n}$.

The naive Kronecker/vec identity $(Z\otimes K)\operatorname{vec}(W)=\operatorname{vec}(KWZ^T)$ followed by masking by $SS^T$ costs $O(nMr)$ — **order $M$, which the problem forbids**.

A correct solution must prove the observed-only contraction. With $a_\ell=\ell\bmod n$, $b_\ell=\lfloor\ell/n\rfloor$ for each observed linear index $\ell$ (column-stack vec layout), set $\tilde K$ = rows $K[a_\ell,:]$, $\tilde Z$ = rows $Z[b_\ell,:]$. Then
$$\mathcal A\operatorname{vec}(W)=\operatorname{vec}\!\big(\tilde K^{T}\operatorname{diag}(s)\,\tilde Z\big)+\lambda\operatorname{vec}(KW),\qquad s=\big((\tilde K W)\odot\tilde Z\big)\mathbf 1\in\mathbb R^{q}.$$
Numerically verified to machine precision ($\sim10^{-13}$) on random instances.

**Required:** a rigorous index/Kronecker proof of this identity, an explicit statement that it touches no $O(M),O(N)$ object, and a careful per-step cost (note $\tilde K^T\operatorname{diag}(s)\tilde Z$ is $q\times n$ times $q\times r$, $O(qnr)$; the often-quoted $O(qr+nr^2)$ appears optimistic unless $K$ is structured — please reconcile).

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Mathematical Review (full analysis)

# Critic Analysis — Q10: Preconditioned CG for RKHS-Constrained CP Decomposition

**Problem type.** This is a *derivation / algorithm-design* problem (numerical linear
algebra), not a classical theorem-proving problem. A complete "proof" must:
(1) derive the normal-equation operator and show it is symmetric positive (semi)definite;
(2) give an *implicit* matrix–vector product that uses only the $q$ observed entries and
the small factors $\tilde K,\tilde Z, K$ (no $O(N)$, no $O(M)$ work);
(3) specify a preconditioner, justify it (SPD, cheap, clusters the spectrum), and analyze
PCG convergence; (4) give a correct complexity count; (5) provide the two required
algorithm boxes (direct symmetric solver + PCG).

All numerical claims below were verified with small random instances (see the run log in
the issue thread): the implicit observed-only matvec matches the explicit operator to
machine precision ($\sim 10^{-13}$).

## Background

**Notation.** $\mathcal T\in\mathbb R^{n_1\times\cdots\times n_d}$, $N=\prod_i n_i$,
$n=n_k$, $M=\prod_{i\ne k} n_i$ (so $N=nM$), $q$ observed entries with $n,r,d<q\ll N$ and
$n\ll M$. $T\in\mathbb R^{n\times M}$ is the mode-$k$ unfolding (zeros for missing),
$Z=A_d\odot\cdots\odot A_1\in\mathbb R^{M\times r}$ (Khatri–Rao, all modes but $k$),
$B=TZ\in\mathbb R^{n\times r}$ the MTTKRP, $K\in\mathbb R^{n\times n}$ psd kernel,
$A_k=KW$, $W\in\mathbb R^{n\times r}$ unknown. $S\in\mathbb R^{N\times q}$ is a column
subset of $I_N$ selecting observed entries of $\operatorname{vec}(T)$.

**System.**
$$\big[(Z\otimes K)^TSS^T(Z\otimes K)+\lambda(I_r\otimes K)\big]\operatorname{vec}(W)
=(I_r\otimes K)\operatorname{vec}(B),$$
of size $nr\times nr$. Denote $\mathcal A=(Z\otimes K)^TSS^T(Z\otimes K)+\lambda(I_r\otimes K)$.

**Key tools.**
- Kronecker/vec identity: $(Z\otimes K)\operatorname{vec}(W)=\operatorname{vec}(KWZ^T)$
  and $(Z\otimes K)^T\operatorname{vec}(V)=\operatorname{vec}(K^TVZ)$
  (standard $\operatorname{vec}(AXB^T)=(B\otimes A)\operatorname{vec}(X)$).
- Mixed-product rule $(A\otimes B)(C\otimes D)=AC\otimes BD$.
- CG convergence: error reduced to $\varepsilon$ in
  $O\!\big(\sqrt{\kappa}\,\log(1/\varepsilon)\big)$ iterations, $\kappa=\lambda_{\max}/\lambda_{\min}$
  of the (preconditioned) operator; finite termination in $\le nr$ steps in exact arithmetic.
- $S$ is a 0/1 selection: $S^Tx$ gathers $q$ entries, $Sy$ scatters them; $SS^T$ is a
  diagonal 0/1 mask of rank $q$.

**Observed-index bookkeeping.** Column-stacking vec of an $n\times M$ matrix maps entry
$(a,b)$ to linear index $a+nb$. So each observed index $\ell$ gives row
$a_\ell=\ell\bmod n\in[n]$ and column $b_\ell=\lfloor \ell/n\rfloor\in[M]$. Define the
$q\times n$ matrix $\tilde K$ with rows $K[a_\ell,:]$ and the $q\times r$ matrix
$\tilde Z$ with rows $Z[b_\ell,:]$ — exactly the $\tilde K,\tilde Z$ named in the prompt.

## Proof Structure

A complete solution must establish, in order:

1. **L1 — Operator identity / SPD.** Derive $\mathcal A$ from the regularized observed least
   squares $\min_W \tfrac12\|S^T\operatorname{vec}(KWZ^T)-S^T\operatorname{vec}(T)\|^2
   +\tfrac\lambda2\langle W,KW\cdot\!\rangle$ (RKHS penalty $\lambda\operatorname{tr}(W^TKW)$),
   show its gradient gives exactly the stated system, and prove $\mathcal A$ is symmetric
   PSD, and SPD when $K\succ0,\lambda>0$. This is what makes CG applicable.

2. **L2 — Implicit matvec (the crux).** Show $u\mapsto\mathcal A u$ with
   $u=\operatorname{vec}(W)$ equals
   $$\operatorname{vec}\Big(\tilde K^{T}\big(\,(\,(\tilde K W)\!*\!*\,)\,\big)\Big)+\lambda\operatorname{vec}(KW),$$
   concretely: (i) $s_\ell=\sum_j (\tilde K W)_{\ell j}\,\tilde Z_{\ell j}$, i.e. the $q$
   observed entries of $KWZ^T$ computed as $s=\big((\tilde K W)\odot\tilde Z\big)\mathbf 1$;
   (ii) form $G\in\mathbb R^{n\times r}$, $G_{pj}=\sum_\ell s_\ell \tilde K_{\ell p}\tilde Z_{\ell j}$,
   i.e. $G=\tilde K^T\big(\operatorname{diag}(s)\tilde Z\big)$; (iii) output $G+\lambda KW$.
   Must prove this equals $\mathcal A\operatorname{vec}(W)$ and uses **no** $O(M),O(N)$ ops.

3. **L3 — RHS without $M,N$.** $B=TZ$ is the precomputed MTTKRP (given). The RHS is
   $\operatorname{vec}(KB)$, cost $O(n^2 r)$ (or $r\cdot\mathrm{cost}(K)$). Must note that
   computing $B$ itself is outside the subproblem (or is the standard sparse MTTKRP on $q$
   nonzeros, $O(qr)$), so the subproblem RHS avoids $O(M),O(N)$.

4. **L4 — Preconditioner.** Choose $\mathcal P$ SPD, cheap to apply, spectrally close to
   $\mathcal A$. Must (a) prove SPD, (b) give matvec cost, (c) argue conditioning.
   *Caveat (see Gap G4):* the naive choice $(Z^TZ+\lambda I_r)\otimes K$ requires forming
   $Z^TZ$ at cost $O(Mr^2)$ — **forbidden**. The admissible analogue uses the observed Gram
   $\tilde Z^T\tilde Z$ ($O(qr^2)$) or a block-Jacobi/diagonal preconditioner built from
   observed quantities.

5. **L5 — Complexity & convergence.** Per-iteration cost
   $O\big(qr+nr^2+r\cdot\mathrm{cost}(K\text{-mult})\big)$ (with $\mathrm{cost}(K)=n^2$ in
   general, or less for structured/Nyström $K$); total
   $O(\#\text{iter}\cdot(\text{per-iter}))$ with $\#\text{iter}=O(\sqrt{\kappa})$. Compare to
   direct $O(n^3r^3)$.

6. **L6 — Algorithm boxes.** Two boxes required: a *direct symmetric* method on the reduced
   observed system and the *PCG* iteration with the implicit matvec and preconditioner.

## Gap Analysis

(No `solution.tex` exists yet; gaps are stated against the proof structure above. Prior
skeleton attempts produced 9 verifier issues, consistent with these gaps.)

- **G1 (L2, central).** Correctness of the *observed-only* implicit matvec must be proved,
  not asserted. The naive vec-identity matvec ($KWZ^T$ then mask) costs $O(nMr)$ —
  **order $M$, forbidden**. The fix is the contraction over the $q$ observed rows
  ($\tilde K,\tilde Z$). A rigorous index proof is required that
  $\mathcal A\operatorname{vec}(W)=\operatorname{vec}\!\big(\tilde K^T\operatorname{diag}(s)\tilde Z\big)+\lambda\operatorname{vec}(KW)$
  with $s=((\tilde K W)\odot\tilde Z)\mathbf 1$. Verified numerically (err $\sim10^{-13}$).

- **G2 (L4, central).** The preconditioner from prior strategy notes,
  $(Z^TZ+\lambda I_r)\otimes K$, **violates the $O(M)$ ban**: forming $Z^TZ$ is $O(Mr^2)$.
  Any admissible solution must replace $Z^TZ$ by an observed quantity (e.g.
  $\tilde Z^T\tilde Z$, cost $O(qr^2)$) and *justify* that the resulting $\mathcal P$ is SPD
  and improves conditioning. This conflation is likely a source of repeated verifier issues.

- **G3 (L1).** SPD must be argued carefully: $\mathcal A\succeq0$ always; strict positivity
  needs $K\succ0$ (the kernel may be only psd / rank-deficient for an RKHS). If $K$ is
  singular, $I_r\otimes K$ is singular and $\mathcal A$ may be singular — CG then needs the
  consistency/range argument or a $K+\epsilon I$ shift. This boundary case must be addressed.

- **G4 (L4, conditioning).** Even with the observed Gram, the claim "PCG converges in few
  iterations" needs a spectral-equivalence argument
  $c_1\mathcal P\preceq\mathcal A\preceq c_2\mathcal P$. With missing data and arbitrary
  $\Omega$ this is *not* automatic; the observed design $\sum_\ell (\tilde Z_\ell\tilde Z_\ell^T)$
  can be ill-conditioned or rank-deficient. The bound on $\kappa(\mathcal P^{-1}\mathcal A)$
  must be stated honestly (it may depend on the coherence/leverage of the observed rows).

- **G5 (L5).** Complexity must separate $\mathrm{cost}(K\text{-multiply})$ (generically
  $O(n^2)$, so the often-quoted $O(qr+nr^2)$ per iteration silently assumes structured $K$).
  Applying the preconditioner needs a factorization of $K$ (Cholesky $O(n^3)$ one-time) and
  of the $r\times r$ Gram — these one-time costs must be counted and shown to beat $O(n^3r^3)$.

- **G6 (L6).** The two algorithm boxes must be self-consistent with the implicit matvec and
  must never instantiate $Z\otimes K$, $T$ (dense), or anything indexed by $M$ or $N$. The
  "direct symmetric" box must operate on the $nr\times nr$ assembled $\mathcal A$ formed via
  the observed contraction (cost $O(qn r^2 + nr\cdot)$ to assemble, then $O(n^3r^3)$ solve)
  — and this assembly itself must avoid $O(M),O(N)$.

## Difficulty Assessment

- **G1** is moderate: a clean index/Kronecker proof, but easy to get the vec layout
  (row/column index split $a=\ell\bmod n$, $b=\lfloor\ell/n\rfloor$) wrong, and easy to
  accidentally reintroduce an $O(M)$ step (`KWZ^T`). The discipline of "observed rows only"
  is the whole point of the problem.
- **G2/G4** are the genuinely hard mathematical content: designing an admissible
  preconditioner that (a) avoids $O(M)$, (b) is provably SPD, (c) yields a *bounded*
  condition number. Spectral equivalence under arbitrary missingness generally fails; an
  honest answer must condition on properties of the observed design or use a randomized /
  block-Jacobi preconditioner with a stated (possibly probabilistic) guarantee.
- **G3** is a standard but necessary boundary argument (psd vs pd kernel).
- **G5** is bookkeeping but frequently botched (hidden $O(n^2)$ in $K$-mult; one-time
  factorization costs).

## Suggested Approaches

1. **Implicit matvec (G1).** State and prove the two-step contraction
   $s=((\tilde K W)\odot\tilde Z)\mathbf 1\in\mathbb R^q$, then
   $G=\tilde K^T\operatorname{diag}(s)\tilde Z=\tilde K^T((s\mathbf 1^T)\odot\tilde Z)$,
   output $\operatorname{vec}(G+\lambda KW)$. Costs: $\tilde K W$ is $O(qnr)$? — note
   $\tilde K$ is $q\times n$ so $\tilde K W$ is $O(qnr)$; better compute $KW$ once
   ($O(n^2r)$) then gather rows $a_\ell$: $O(qr)$. Then $s$: $O(qr)$; $G$: $O(qnr)$ via
   $\tilde K^T(\cdot)$ — to hit $O(qr+nr^2)$ one must be careful, may need
   $\hat G=\tilde Z^T\operatorname{diag}? $ Reconsider order: form $H=\operatorname{diag}(s)\tilde Z$
   ($O(qr)$), then $G=\tilde K^T H$ is $q\times n$ times $q\times r$ → $O(qnr)$. So the
   honest per-iter cost is $O(qnr + n^2 r)$ unless $K$ is structured. **The solver should
   recheck the advertised $O(qr+nr^2)$ — it appears optimistic.** (Flag: verify.)

2. **Preconditioner (G2/G4).** Use the *separable observed* preconditioner
   $\mathcal P=(\tilde Z^T\tilde Z+\lambda I_r)\otimes K$ (apply via solving the $r\times r$
   system and one $K$-solve through a one-time Cholesky $K=LL^T$). Prove SPD when $K\succ0$.
   For conditioning, prove the spectral-equivalence
   $\mathcal A=\sum_\ell (\tilde Z_\ell\tilde Z_\ell^T)\otimes(\tilde K_\ell\tilde K_\ell^T)+\lambda(I_r\otimes K)$
   and bound $\mathcal P^{-1}\mathcal A$ using $0\preceq \tilde K_\ell\tilde K_\ell^T\preceq \|\tilde K_\ell\|^2 I$;
   give the bound explicitly (likely $\kappa\le 1+\max_\ell\|K[a_\ell,:]\|^2 \cdot(\ldots)/\lambda$).
   Alternatively block-Jacobi: the $n$ diagonal $r\times r$ blocks of $\mathcal A$
   (one per row of $W$), each invertible at $O(r^3)$, total $O(nr^3)$ once.
3. **SPD/boundary (G3).** Add $K\leftarrow K+\epsilon I$ or argue on $\operatorname{range}(K)$;
   state assumption $K\succ0$ explicitly.
4. **Algorithm boxes (G6).** Direct: assemble $\mathcal A$ by accumulating
   $\sum_\ell (\tilde Z_\ell\tilde Z_\ell^T)\otimes(\tilde K_\ell\tilde K_\ell^T)$
   ($O(qn^2r^2)$ assembly) + $O(n^3r^3)$ solve; PCG: standard preconditioned CG with the
   matvec of (1) and preconditioner of (2).

## References

Prior docs read: `docs_overview.md` (background, candidate answer, boundary cases),
`docs_progress.md` (status: 9 failed skeleton attempts, 9 verifier issues each),
`docs_strategies.md` (the $(Z^TZ+\lambda I_r)\otimes K$ preconditioner suggestion — flagged
here as $O(M)$-violating), `docs_timeline.md` (attempt history).
Theorems/tools cited: vec–Kronecker identity $\operatorname{vec}(AXB^T)=(B\otimes A)\operatorname{vec}(X)$;
mixed-product rule; conjugate-gradient convergence theorem
($O(\sqrt\kappa\log1/\varepsilon)$); Cholesky factorization; Nyström kernel approximation;
RKHS representer theorem.

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Mathematical Review (full analysis attached as doc)

# Critic Analysis — Problem 10 (appliedmath_rm14k / q10)
## Preconditioned CG for RKHS-Constrained CP Decomposition

## Background

**Setting.** We are inside an alternating-optimization (ALS) sweep for a CP
tensor decomposition of rank $r$ of a $d$-way tensor
$\mathcal T\in\mathbb R^{n_1\times\cdots\times n_d}$ with *missing entries*.
For the mode-$k$ subproblem all other factor matrices are fixed and we solve for
the mode-$k$ factor $A_k$. Mode $k$ is infinite-dimensional / RKHS-constrained,
which is modeled by writing $A_k=KW$ with $K\in\mathbb R^{n\times n}$ the (PSD)
RKHS kernel (Gram) matrix and $W\in\mathbb R^{n\times r}$ the unknown.

**Notation.**
- $N=\prod_i n_i$, $n=n_k$, $M=\prod_{i\ne k}n_i$, $n\ll M$.
- $q\ll N$ observed entries; $T\in\mathbb R^{n\times M}$ is the mode-$k$
  unfolding with missing entries zeroed.
- $Z=A_d\odot\cdots\odot A_{k+1}\odot A_{k-1}\odot\cdots\odot A_1\in\mathbb R^{M\times r}$
  is the Khatri–Rao product of the other factors.
- $S\in\mathbb R^{N\times q}$ is a column-subset of $I_N$; $S^T\vecop(T)$ selects the
  $q$ observed entries.
- $B=TZ$ is the MTTKRP.

**The normal equations.** The mode-$k$ subproblem is the regularized
least-squares system
$$
\underbrace{\Big[(Z\otimes K)^T S\,S^T(Z\otimes K)+\lambda(I_r\otimes K)\Big]}_{=:A}
\,\vecop(W)=(I_r\otimes K)\,\vecop(B).
$$
This is $nr\times nr$. A dense factorization costs $O(n^3r^3)$, and even forming
$A$ explicitly is expensive. The task: solve it with **preconditioned conjugate
gradient (PCG)**, computing all matvecs *implicitly* using only $O(\cdot)$ in
$n,r,q$ (never $O(M)$ or $O(N)$), give a preconditioner with a convergence
guarantee, and present **two algorithm boxes** (a direct symmetric method and a
PCG method).

**Key algebraic tools.**
- Vectorization identity (column-major): $(B^T\otimes A)\vecop(X)=\vecop(AXB)$.
  Hence $(Z\otimes K)\vecop(W)=\vecop(KWZ^T)$ and $(I_r\otimes K)\vecop(W)=\vecop(KW)$.
- $S S^T$ is the diagonal $0/1$ projector onto the observed index set in
  $\mathbb R^N$; in unfolding coordinates it zeroes all but the $q$ observed
  $(i,j)$ entries of an $n\times M$ matrix.
- Khatri–Rao structure: row $j$ of $Z$ is the Hadamard product of the
  corresponding rows of the fixed factors, computable on demand in $O(dr)$.

## Proof Structure

A complete solution must establish, with full proofs and the two required
algorithm boxes:

1. **Implicit LHS matvec.** Show $A\,\vecop(W)$ can be evaluated using only the
   $q$ observed index pairs $(i_\ell,j_\ell)$ and the corresponding rows
   $K(i_\ell,:)$, $Z(j_\ell,:)$, in cost $O(n^2r+qnr)$, with **no** $O(M)$ or
   $O(N)$ object ever materialized.
2. **Implicit RHS / MTTKRP.** Show $B=TZ$ and the RHS $(I_r\otimes K)\vecop(B)
   =\vecop(KB)$ can be formed observed-only in $O(qr+n^2r)$.
3. **Operator is SPD on the solve space** so CG/PCG is applicable; treat the case
   where $K$ is only PSD (singular).
4. **Preconditioner** that (a) is $O(M)/O(N)$-free to build and apply and (b) is
   provably spectrally equivalent to $A$, giving a condition-number /
   iteration-count bound.
5. **Two algorithm boxes** (direct symmetric; PCG) with Input/Output, pseudocode,
   correctness, termination, and complexity, all $O(M)/O(N)$-free.

## Gap Analysis

(No `solution.tex` exists yet; gaps are derived from the problem statement.)

The four core sub-lemmas are **already tracked as open issues**:
- **q10-2** Implicit observed-only LHS matvec (avoid $O(M)$ $KWZ^T$).
- **q10-3** Preconditioner choice that avoids the $O(M)$ ban.
- **q10-5** Condition-number / spectral-equivalence bound for PCG convergence.
- **q10-7** Two algorithm boxes must avoid any $O(M)/O(N)$ object.

This review verified the underlying mathematics numerically (see below) and
identifies **two additional sub-lemmas not yet covered** by the open issues:

- **GAP A — Observed-only MTTKRP / RHS construction.** The right-hand side
  $(I_r\otimes K)\vecop(B)=\vecop(KB)$ requires $B=TZ$. The naive MTTKRP is
  $O(nMr)$ ($O(M)$ — banned). A correct solution must prove that, since $T$ has
  only $q$ nonzeros, $B$ is assembled as $B(i,:)=\sum_{j:(i,j)\text{ obs}}T(i,j)\,Z(j,:)$
  in $O(qr)$, then $KB$ in $O(n^2r)$. This is a *distinct* object from the LHS
  matvec (q10-2) and is required for the PCG right-hand side; it has no open
  issue.

- **GAP B — SPD justification for CG, including singular $K$.** CG/PCG require
  the system operator to be symmetric positive (semi)definite on the iteration
  subspace. $A=(\,\cdot\,)^T(\cdot)+\lambda(I_r\otimes K)$ is symmetric PSD, but
  $K$ is only assumed PSD, so $A$ can be singular (null space
  $\{\vecop(W):KW=0\text{ and }S^T(Z\otimes K)\vecop(W)=0\}$). One must prove
  either (i) $K\succ0$ (e.g. with the $+\lambda$ regularization restricted to the
  range of $K$) so that $A\succ0$, or (ii) that CG converges on the consistent
  system within $\mathrm{range}(A)$ and the recovered $A_k=KW$ is well-defined
  even if $W$ is not unique. This well-posedness step is a genuine gap with no
  open issue.

## Difficulty Assessment

- **q10-2 / GAP A (matvecs):** Conceptually moderate but error-prone. The
  vec/Kronecker bookkeeping and the column-major vs. row-major unfolding
  convention are easy to get wrong; the scatter step $A\,\vecop(W)\ni
  \sum_\ell s_\ell\,K(:,i_\ell)Z(j_\ell,:)$ must touch only observed indices to
  stay $O(qnr)$. A naive `K @ G @ Z` with $G\in\mathbb R^{n\times M}$ silently
  reintroduces $O(M)$.
- **q10-3 (preconditioner build):** The "natural" Tikhonov preconditioner
  $(Z^TZ+\lambda I_r)\otimes K$ requires $Z^TZ$ which is $O(Mr^2)$ — banned.
  Producing an $O(M)$-free, *data-dependent* (observed Gram) preconditioner that
  is still effective is the crux.
- **q10-5 (convergence bound):** Hard. One must bound $\kappa(P^{-1}A)$ via a
  spectral-equivalence inequality $c_1P\preceq A\preceq c_2P$. The constants
  depend on the leverage/coherence of the observed pattern and on $\lambda$;
  proving uniform-in-$M$ bounds is the deepest part.
- **GAP B (SPD/singular $K$):** Moderate but subtle; must avoid the false claim
  "$A\succ0$" when $K$ is merely PSD.
- **q10-7 (algorithm boxes):** Mechanical given 1–4, but must faithfully encode
  the $O$-bounds and never reference $T$, $Z\otimes K$, or $B$ as dense $M$-sized
  objects.

## Suggested Approaches

**Implicit LHS matvec (verified numerically here).**
Using $(Z\otimes K)\vecop(W)=\vecop(KWZ^T)$ and that $SS^T$ keeps only observed
$(i,j)$:
1. Precompute $KW\in\mathbb R^{n\times r}$ — $O(n^2r)$.
2. For each observed pair $(i_\ell,j_\ell)$: $s_\ell=(KW)(i_\ell,:)\cdot Z(j_\ell,:)^T$ — $O(qr)$.
3. Scatter: $H=\sum_\ell s_\ell\,K(:,i_\ell)\,Z(j_\ell,:)\in\mathbb R^{n\times r}$ — $O(qnr)$.
4. Output $\vecop(H+\lambda KW)$.
Total $O(n^2r+qnr)$, no $O(M)$. (Numerically confirmed to equal $A\vecop(W)$.)

**Implicit RHS (GAP A, verified).**
$B(i,:)=\sum_{j:(i,j)\text{ obs}}T(i,j)Z(j,:)$ in $O(qr)$; then $KB$ in $O(n^2r)$.

**Preconditioner (q10-3).** Use the *observed* Gram instead of $Z^TZ$:
e.g. an approximation $A\approx \hat G$ where $\hat G$ aggregates observed-row
outer products of $Z$, combined with $K$ as $P=(\hat\Gamma+\lambda I_r)\otimes K$
with $\hat\Gamma$ built from observed rows only, or a block-Jacobi/Kronecker-mean
factorization. Apply $P^{-1}$ via small $r\times r$ and $n\times n$ solves
(eigendecompose $K$ once, $O(n^3)$ amortized over CG iterations and ALS sweeps).
$\lambda(I_r\otimes K)$ alone already reduces $\kappa$ by $\sim$2 orders of
magnitude in the numerical test, but a Kronecker preconditioner including the
data term is needed for $M$-independent bounds.

**Convergence bound (q10-5).** Prove $c_1P\preceq A\preceq c_2P$ and invoke the
standard CG bound: error after $t$ steps $\le 2\big((\sqrt\kappa-1)/(\sqrt\kappa+1)\big)^t$
with $\kappa=c_2/c_1$.

**SPD (GAP B).** Show $A\succeq\lambda(I_r\otimes K)\succeq0$; if $K\succ0$ then
$A\succ0$ and CG is well-posed. If $K$ is singular, restrict to $\mathrm{range}(K)$
(the RKHS quotient), where $A_k=KW$ depends only on $KW$, and run CG on the
consistent reduced system.

## Numerical Verification Performed

- `(Z⊗K)vecW = vec(KWZ^T)` and `(I_r⊗K)vecW = vec(KW)`: confirmed.
- Observed-only LHS matvec equals dense $A\vecop(W)$: confirmed (random
  $n,M,r,q,\lambda$).
- Observed-only MTTKRP $B=TZ$ equals dense product: confirmed.
- $\kappa(A)\approx2.6\times10^4$ vs. $\kappa(P^{-1}A)\approx1.3\times10^2$ for
  $P=\lambda(I_r\otimes K)$: confirmed preconditioning is essential.

## References

- Problem statement: `problems/q10` (Kolda & Ward, "Preconditioned CG for
  RKHS-Constrained CP Decomposition").
- Skill guidance: `skills/math-research/SKILL.md` (Algorithm Rule, Gap audit
  taxonomy F1–F4).
- Standard tools: vectorization/Kronecker identities; Khatri–Rao/MTTKRP
  (Kolda–Bader, *Tensor Decompositions and Applications*, SIAM Review 2009);
  CG convergence bound (Golub–Van Loan, *Matrix Computations*); spectral
  equivalence preconditioning.
- Existing open issues reviewed: q10-2, q10-3, q10-5, q10-7.
